calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-02-2026 Continued To Oct-28-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings
Off Calendar Mar-19-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Marc Litton, Esq. 415-421-4774 Law Offices for Plaintiff.
Roderick Bushnell, Esq. 415-217-3800 LAw Offices for Plaintiff.
Anastasia Osbrink, Esq. 619-515-1899 Littler Mendelson for Defendant.
Court reporter: Teckla Clay Pratt, CSR #13125, 310-490-4905, tecklapratt@gmail.com

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.
Ms. Osbrink contests and presents argument; arbitration agreement not enforceable; cites case authority; intertwining; cure is to order to arbitration and stay the case.
Court comments and poses questions.
Mr. Litton responds; alter ego issue; CCP 1281.2(c);
Ms. Osbrink presents a brief rebuttal

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Align Residential Management, LLC's Motion to Compel Arbitration.

Taken Under Submission.***

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

A proposed order is submitted for the court's review and signature.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Teckla Clay Pratt, CSR #13125, 310-490-4905, tecklapratt@gmail.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an order. (see scanned order for details)***

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings
Off Calendar Feb-25-2026 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings
Off Calendar Feb-19-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Thomas Marc Litton, Esq. 415-421-4774 Law Offices for Plaintiff.
John Nordlund, Esq.619-515-1823 Littler Mendelson for Defendant.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received and was informed there was an amended notive filed.

ARGUED. Mr. Nordlund presents arguments; apologizes to the court, conflict in the office, administrative error, oversight, will deal with issue internally.
Court comments and gives admonishment; no courtesy copies for the March hearing, waste of public resources, can call or send an email.
Mr. Litton has nothing further to add.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendants' unopposed Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration.

The tentative ruling is vacated. The matter is off-calendar. Mr. Nordlund to lodge courtesy copies to Dept. 302 for the 3/19 hearing promptly.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
